person.name: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington

nameForms.fullText: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Le livre, The Battle Abbey roll : with some account of the Norman lineages, volume 3, (1889), édité par Catherine Lucy Wilhelmina Powlett Cleveland (duchesse de), publié par J. Murray, p. 26, dit ce qui suit au sujet de la famille Placy:

« Placy. Pour Placetis, une famille baronniale qui s’est largement répandue. Mais leur nom n’a pas de titre pour être ici, car ils descendent de Jean de Placetis ou de Plessetis, un Normand de naissance, qui, d’être un domestique à la cour d’Henri III. , s’est élevé en faveur de son maître, a été rapidement promu à la richesse et au pouvoir, et est finalement devenu comte de Warwick par son mariage avec Margery de Newburgh. Elle était, comme nous l’avons vu, une épouse très réticente, car, bien que, après la mort de son premier mari, John Mareschal, le roi a émis son mandat qu’elle devrait être «sincèrement persuadé—pour ne pas dire, commandé—d’épouser cet aventurier de bas-né, il semble lui-même avoir douté de son obéissance;» et, le lendemain de Noël, promis à son favori, au cas où on ne pourrait l’inciter à obéir, l’amende qu’elle aurait à payer pour se marier sans le consentement royal. » (source : http://books.google.ca/books?id=FF8JAAAAIAJ&pg=PA26&lpg=PA26&dq=%22the+King+issued+his+mandate+that+s)

sourceList.title: Rootsweb: The Phillips, Weber, Kirk, & Staggs families of the Pacific Northwest

zh.Kidlington, Oxfordshire, England: 英國奧可福爾吉德林頓
zh.Le Plessix, Morbihan, Brittany, France: 法國布列塔尼莫比韓勒 普萊希可
zh.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington於大約1177年出生在法國布列塔尼莫比韓勒 普萊希可。他和Margery de Newburgh至少有3個兒子。他於1207年被列為in the royal service。他去世於1227年在英國奧可福爾吉德林頓享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington于大约1177年出生在法国布列塔尼莫比韓勒 普萊希可。他和Margery de Newburgh至少有3个儿子。他于1207年被列为in the royal service。他去世于1227年在英国奧可福爾吉德林頓享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: John

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington was born about 1177, in Le Plessix, Morbihan, Brittany, France. He had at least 3 sons with Margery de Newburgh. In 1207, at the age of 31, his occupation is listed as in the royal service. He died in 1227, in Kidlington, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington est né environ 1177, à Le Plessix, Morbihan, Bretagne, France. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Margery de Newburgh. En 1207, à l’âge de 31 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est in the royal service. Il est décédé en 1227, à Kidlington, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington wurde um 1177 in Le Plessix, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Margery de Newburgh mindestens 3 Söhne. 1207, als er 31 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als in the royal service aufgeführt. Er starb 1227 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Kidlington, Oxfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington è nato il circa 1177 nel Le Plessix, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Margery de Newburgh. Nel 1207, all'età di 31 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come in the royal service. Morì nel 1227, nel Kidlington, Oxfordshire, England, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.Le Plessix, Morbihan, Brittany, France: フランスブレタグンモービハンル プレシックス
ja.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlingtonは約1177年にフランスブレタグンモービハンル プレシックスで生まれた。彼はMargery de Newburghとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。1207年，31歳の時，職業はin the royal serviceと記録されている。彼は1227年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアキドリントンで51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington(은)는 대략 1177년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 일 플레시스에서 태어났다. 그는 Margery de Newburgh(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1207년, 31세의 나이로 그의 직업은 in the royal service이었다. 그는 1227년 영국 스퍼드실르 키들링턴에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington nasceu aproximadamente 1177, em Le Plessix, Morbihan, Bretagne, França. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Margery de Newburgh. Em 1207, com 31 anos, sua profissão aparece como in the royal service. Ele faleceu em 1227, em Kidlington, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington родился около 1177 г. в местности л плессикс, морбихань, бретагн, Франция. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Margery de Newburgh. В 1207 году, в возрасте 31 год,  он работал по профессии in the royal service. Он умер в 1227 г. в местности кидлингтон, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: John du Plessis I, of Kidlington nació aproximadamente 1177, en Le Plessix, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Margery de Newburgh. En 1207, a la edad de 31 años, su ocupación registrada era in the royal service. Murió en 1227, en Kidlington, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 51 años.
